relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Sabbath hunger meets grainfield provision.
The question becomes lawfulness on Sabbath.
Jesus remembers David’s hunger.
Sacred bread served need beyond ordinary boundary.
The Son of Humanity is lord of Sabbath.
Another Sabbath brings a withered hand into the synagogue.
Watchers turn healing into accusation.
Jesus knows the reasoning and brings the man to the middle.
Sabbath is asked to choose good, life, and restoration.
The hand stretches and is restored.
Healing exposes fury in closed hearts.
Jesus spends the night in mountain prayer.
From prayer, the Twelve are chosen and named sent ones.
The circle begins with Simon Peter and Andrew.
The circle continues through named disciples.
The circle includes the betrayer too.
Jesus stands on a level place with disciples and multitudes.
People come to hear, heal, and be released.
Power flows through touch to heal all.
The poor are blessed because the Divine reign is theirs.
Hunger and weeping are held inside promised reversal.
Rejection because of the Son of Humanity is named blessing.
Joy leaps with prophetic solidarity.
The rich receive a woe because consolation is already consumed.
Fullness and laughter without mercy turn toward hunger and mourning.
Universal praise can mark false prophecy.
Those who hear are called into enemy-love.
Blessing and prayer answer curse and mistreatment.
Retaliation is interrupted.
Grasping is loosened by giving.
The rule is mutuality: do as you desire to receive.
Love only for lovers is not yet grace.
Good only for good is ordinary reciprocity.
Lending only for return remains transactional.
Enemy-love imitates the Most High’s kindness.
Mercy is the family resemblance of the Father.
Judgment and condemnation are released through release.
Measure returns overflowing.
Blind guidance leads both into a pit.
The fully formed disciple becomes like the teacher.
The speck is easier to see than the beam.
Clear sight begins with one’s own beam.
Fruit reveals the tree.
Each life is known by what it bears.
The mouth speaks from the heart’s abundance.
Calling Lord without doing is exposed.
Coming, hearing, and doing make the foundation.
Deep foundation on rock withstands flood.
Hearing without doing leaves the house foundationless.
